title: Survey Responses
excerpt: I created a customer satisfaction survey in our service board, and I want the responses to be shown on the item from which they were sent and not create a new item. I’ve added a tag on the end of the survey link, but it …

posts.author: BradyChristian (Brady Christian)
posts.cookedHtml:
I created a customer satisfaction survey in our service board, and I want the responses to be shown on the item from which they were sent and not create a new item. I’ve added a tag on the end of the survey link, but it just creates a new item with the name of the original item.

Is there a way to do what I want?

posts.author: Tom-CXmonday (Tom)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Brady, Tom from monday’s Customer Experience here Currently, our WorkForms can only create new items, and not update existing ones, but I think we might be able to achieve a similar goal to what you’re looking to achieve with connect boards type columns/questions. This type of question allows the new items created by a form to be connected to existing ones and mirror details between them. Here’s an article from our knowledge base on that: Connect Boards question

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
Which product (CRM/Work Management, etc) and level (Pro/Enterprise) do you have?

Desiree - www.thecleverclovers.com

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: You’ll need to look at a 3rd party app then ie SuperForm, it allows for that functionality. I asked which tier, because if you had Enterprise, you get a few more features on workforms.

posts.author: noam.lapidot (Noam Lapidot)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @BradyChristian If you are open to aarketplace app, you can easily do that with SuperForm. Here’s how:

Update Existing Items Using monday.com Forms https://share.google/QgCV8ECriqrU6irAP
